headline: How The Vår Energi (OB:VAR) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Targets And Guidance
summary: Vår Energi’s updated fair value estimate has moved from NOK 38.93 to NOK 44.72, putting a fresh spotlight on what analysts think the shares are worth today. That shift sits against a split analyst backdrop, with some targets in the NOK 39 to NOK 44 range and others much lower, reflecting different views on valuation and execution risk. As you read on, you will see how these price targets are being set and how to track the story as it evolves. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the...

headline: Is the world running out of oil? Goldman Sachs weighs in
summary: Investing.com – Fears that the world may be running out of oil have resurfaced as the ongoing Middle East conflict disrupts flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical artery for global energy trade. However, a new analysis from Goldman Sachs suggests that while supply chains are under severe strain, the situation is more nuanced than an outright global shortage.

headline: How Fed Rate Cut Uncertainty Is Shaping The Evolving Story For Econocom Group (ENXTBR:ECONB)
summary: Econocom Group’s fair value estimate holds steady at €1.94 per share, with no change to the headline price target in the latest analyst update. That stability comes as major banks, including Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and Barclays, update their views on Federal Reserve rate cuts, which feeds directly into how discount rates and long term cash flows are framed in current models. As you read on, you will see how these moving macro pieces shape the evolving narrative around Econocom Group and what...

headline: Chinese Bonds Near Inflection Point as Inflation Outlook Shifts
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Chinese bonds may be reaching a historical turning point, with yields set to climb from record low levels as deflationary pressures ease and expectations for monetary loosening recede.The benchmark 10-year yield has the potential to finally break out of its recent narrow trading range and rise toward 2% or even higher this year from around 1.8% now, some analysts say.
